 {"id":"bd-4enj","title":"Scheduler + alert execution for saved searches","description":"## What
Add a background scheduler to execute saved searches and emit alerts.

## Why
Scheduled alerts turn wa into a proactive monitor rather than a pull-only tool.

## How
- Integrate with watcher runtime loop or dedicated task
- Enforce max frequency + backoff
- Emit notification events with redacted snippets + counts

## Risks
- Alert storm risk; must rate-limit and include dedupe window

## Success Criteria
- Scheduled searches run on interval
- Alerts include query name, scope, and match count","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:42.921020652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:37.254176019Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4enj","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:42.933130217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4enj","depends_on_id":"bd-2uyz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:37.254109645Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4enj","depends_on_id":"bd-yt9x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:26.769106852Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-5wge","title":"Secret scan engine (reuse redactor patterns)","description":"## What
Implement a scanner that reuses redaction patterns to detect secrets in stored segments.

## Why
We need a deterministic, safe way to assess exposure without printing secrets.

## How
- Stream segments from storage, run redaction patterns
- Count matches and record redacted snippets (hashes only)

## Success Criteria
- Scanner never emits raw secret values
- Performance is acceptable on large DBs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:20.128394062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:20.146639119Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5wge","depends_on_id":"bd-2yoy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:20.146556946Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-jfkk","title":"Audit and enhance ingest loop to provide all status metadata without Lua","description":"## Overview

Verify that wa's existing ingest/polling loop already provides all the metadata that STATUS_UPDATE_LUA was sending, and enhance it if needed. The goal is to confirm we lose nothing by removing the Lua hook.

## Current STATUS_UPDATE_LUA Payload

The Lua snippet sends this JSON to \`wa event --from-status\`:
\`\`\`json
{
  "schema_version": 0,
  "pane_id": 123,
  "domain": "local",
  "title": "zsh",
  "cursor": {"row": 10, "col": 5},
  "dimensions": {"rows": 24, "cols": 80},
  "is_alt_screen": false,
  "ts": 1706123456789
}
\`\`\`

## What Ingest Loop Already Provides

wa's ingest loop (crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs) polls \`wezterm cli list --format json\`:

\`\`\`json
[
  {
    "window_id": 0,
    "tab_id": 0,
    "pane_id": 0,
    "workspace": "default",
    "size": "80x24",        // ✅ dimensions available
    "title": "zsh",         // ✅ title available  
    "cwd": "/home/user",    // ✅ cwd available (bonus!)
    "domain": "local",      // ✅ domain available (new in WezTerm)
    "is_active": true       // ✅ active state available
  }
]
\`\`\`

## Gap Analysis

| Field | Lua Provides | CLI Provides | Action Needed |
|-------|-------------|--------------|---------------|
| pane_id | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| domain | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| title | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| dimensions | ✅ (rows/cols) | ✅ (80x24 string) | Parse "80x24" → (80, 24) |
| cursor position | ✅ | ❌ | See notes below |
| is_alt_screen | ✅ | ❌ | Handled by bd-2v4t (escape sequence detection) |
| cwd | ❌ | ✅ | Bonus - we get MORE data |

### Cursor Position

The cursor position was rarely used by wa. Options:
1. **Remove dependency**: Confirm no wa code actually needs cursor position
2. **On-demand query**: If ever needed, \`wezterm cli get-text --pane-id X\` includes cursor info
3. **Escape sequence parsing**: Could track cursor from CSI sequences (complex, probably overkill)

**Recommendation**: Audit wa codebase to confirm cursor position is unused, document as unavailable in polling mode.

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Parse Dimensions String
\`\`\`rust
fn parse_dimensions(size: &str) -> Option<(u16, u16)> {
    let parts: Vec<&str> = size.split('x').collect();
    if parts.len() == 2 {
        let cols = parts[0].parse().ok()?;
        let rows = parts[1].parse().ok()?;
        Some((cols, rows))
    } else {
        None
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Verify Domain Field Available
Check WezTerm version requirements — \`domain\` field was added in WezTerm 20230712+.
Add fallback for older versions (default to "local").

### 3. Audit Cursor Usage
Search codebase for any dependency on cursor position:
\`\`\`bash
rg "cursor" crates/wa-core/src/
\`\`\`

### 4. Update PaneInfo Struct
Ensure ingest.rs PaneInfo struct includes all fields:
\`\`\`rust
pub struct PaneInfo {
    pub pane_id: u64,
    pub title: String,
    pub domain: String,
    pub cwd: Option<String>,
    pub dimensions: (u16, u16),  // (cols, rows)
    pub is_active: bool,
    pub is_alt_screen: bool,  // From ScreenStateTracker
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All STATUS_UPDATE_LUA fields available without Lua (except cursor if unused)
- [ ] Dimensions parsed from "WxH" string format
- [ ] Domain field handled with fallback for old WezTerm
- [ ] Cursor position usage audited and documented
- [ ] PaneInfo struct updated
- [ ] Unit tests for dimension parsing
- [ ] Integration test: ingest loop provides complete metadata

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-2v4t (alt-screen detection) for is_alt_screen field

## Files to Modify

- crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs — PaneInfo struct and parsing
- crates/wa-core/src/wezterm.rs — CLI output parsing

## References

- wezterm cli list: https://wezfurlong.org/wezterm/cli/cli/list.html
- Current ingest.rs: crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:44:26.071486794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:51.670537304Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:51.670460841Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jfkk","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:44:26.079402073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-jfkk","depends_on_id":"bd-2v4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:44:35.987984256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":75,"issue_id":"bd-jfkk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Audit Complete - All Metadata Already Available

### Findings:

**1. Cursor Position** ✅
- `PaneInfo` struct already has `cursor_x: Option<u32>` and `cursor_y: Option<u32>`
- Populated by `wezterm cli list --format json`
- Test at wezterm.rs:1284-1285 confirms parsing works

**2. Dimensions** ✅
- `PaneInfo.size` has rows/cols (nested struct)
- Fallback to `PaneInfo.rows`/`PaneInfo.cols` (flat fields)
- `effective_rows()`/`effective_cols()` methods provide unified access

**3. Alt-Screen** ✅ (escape sequence detection ALREADY EXISTS)
- `PaneCursor.in_alt_screen` is updated during `capture_snapshot()`
- Uses `detect_alt_screen_changes()` at ingest.rs:1573 to find ESC[?1049h/l sequences
- When Lua removed: use `cursor.in_alt_screen` as canonical source

**4. Domain, Title, CWD** ✅
- `PaneInfo.domain_name`, `PaneInfo.title`, `PaneInfo.cwd` all populated from CLI
- `effective_domain()`, `effective_title()`, `parsed_cwd()` helper methods exist

### Action Needed:
None for data availability. The next task (bd-30gj) can safely remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA since all metadata is already available via CLI + escape sequence detection.

### Note on Two Alt-Screen Sources:
Currently IPC returns both:
- `alt_screen`: From PaneEntry.is_alt_screen (Lua IPC source - will become stale)
- `cursor_alt_screen`: From PaneCursor.in_alt_screen (escape sequence detection)

After Lua removal, consumers should use `cursor_alt_screen` as the authoritative source.","created_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:40Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mwst","title":"Document Lua removal: CHANGELOG, migration guide, architecture update","description":"## Overview

Update all documentation to reflect the removal of STATUS_UPDATE_LUA and the new polling-based architecture. This ensures users understand the change and can migrate smoothly.

## Documentation Updates Required

### 1. CHANGELOG.md

Add entry for the version that removes STATUS_UPDATE_LUA:

\`\`\`markdown
## [0.2.0] - 2026-XX-XX

### Changed

- **BREAKING**: Removed Lua-based status update hook (\`update-status\`)
  - Dramatically improves WezTerm performance by eliminating high-frequency Lua callbacks
  - Pane metadata now obtained via polling (\`wezterm cli list\`)
  - Alt-screen detection now via escape sequence parsing (more reliable)

### Removed

- \`wa event --from-status\` CLI command (internal, not public API)
- STATUS_UPDATE_LUA snippet from \`wa setup\` output

### Migration

If you previously ran \`wa setup\`, run it again to update your wezterm.lua:

\`\`\`bash
wa setup --wezterm
\`\`\`

This will automatically remove the deprecated Lua code from the WA-managed block.
\`\`\`

### 2. README.md / AGENTS.md

Update any references to status updates:

- Remove mentions of "real-time status updates"
- Update architecture diagram if it shows Lua→wa flow
- Add note about polling-based pane metadata

### 3. PLAN.md / PLAN_CODEX.md

Update the specification:

**Section to update:**
- §2.7 (Status update specification) — mark as deprecated/removed
- Appendix E.1 — remove STATUS_UPDATE_LUA documentation

**Add new section:**
- §X.X Polling-based pane metadata
- Explain how alt-screen detection works via escape sequences

### 4. Architecture Documentation

Create or update architecture docs explaining:

1. **Before (Lua-based)**:
   \`\`\`
   WezTerm update-status event (60Hz)
     → Lua handler (runs every event)
       → Rate limit check
         → JSON serialization
           → wezterm.background_child_process
             → wa event --from-status
               → IPC to watcher
   \`\`\`

2. **After (Polling-based)**:
   \`\`\`
   wa watcher ingest loop (configurable interval)
     → wezterm cli list --format json
       → Parse pane metadata
     → ScreenStateTracker.process_output()
       → Parse alt-screen escape sequences
   \`\`\`

### 5. Inline Code Documentation

Update doc comments in affected files:

- crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs: Update module-level docs
- crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs: Document polling-based metadata
- crates/wa-core/src/screen_state.rs: Document escape sequence detection

### 6. Migration Guide

Create a dedicated migration section (can be in README or separate file):

\`\`\`markdown
## Migrating from wa 0.1.x to 0.2.x

### What Changed

In 0.2.0, we removed the Lua-based status update hook to dramatically improve WezTerm performance.

### Impact

- **No action required** for most users
- If you have custom integrations with \`wa event --from-status\`, see below

### Steps to Upgrade

1. **Update wa** to the latest version
2. **Re-run setup** to update your wezterm.lua:
   \`\`\`bash
   wa setup --wezterm
   \`\`\`
3. **Restart WezTerm** to pick up config changes

### Verifying the Migration

After upgrading, your wezterm.lua's WA-managed block should NOT contain:
- \`wezterm.on('update-status'\`
- \`wa_last_status_update\`
- \`WA_STATUS_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS\`

It SHOULD still contain:
- \`wezterm.on('user-var-changed'\` (for agent signaling)

### Breaking Changes

- \`wa event --from-status\` command removed
- StatusUpdate IPC message type removed
- These were internal implementation details, not public APIs
\`\`\`

- [ ] README.md updated (if applicable)
- [ ] AGENTS.md updated (if applicable)

- [ ] Migration guide written
- [ ] Code doc comments updated
- [ ] All docs spell-checked and links verified

- Depends on: bd-o2h4 (E2E tests passing, feature complete)

## Files to Modify/Create

- CHANGELOG.md
- README.md (if applicable)
- AGENTS.md (if applicable)
- PLAN.md
- PLAN_CODEX.md
- docs/migration-0.2.md (new, optional)
- crates/wa-core/src/setup.rs (doc comments)
- crates/wa-core/src/ingest.rs (doc comments)

## References

- Existing PLAN.md: project root
- WezTerm docs style: https://wezfurlong.org/wezterm/","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"LilacMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:47:14.119123581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:14:18.692990390Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:14:18.692830854Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mwst","depends_on_id":"bd-21ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:17.722732220Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwst","depends_on_id":"bd-2l5s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:47:14.130330427Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwst","depends_on_id":"bd-o2h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:47:23.903246697Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"wa-y6g","title":"Schema migration framework: versioned migrations with rollback support","description":"# Task: Schema Migration Framework

## Goal
Enable wa to safely upgrade its database schema across versions without data loss.

## Why This Matters
As wa evolves:
- New columns/tables needed for features
- Indexes may change for performance
- Data format may evolve

Without migrations, users would need to:
- Delete database and lose history
- Manually transform data

## Design

### Migration Files
```
src/storage/migrations/
├── mod.rs
├── v001_initial_schema.rs
├── v002_add_event_metadata.rs
├── v003_fts_improvements.rs
└── v004_workflow_state.rs
```

### Migration Structure
```rust
pub struct Migration {
    pub version: u32,
    pub name: &'static str,
    pub up: fn(&Connection) -> Result<()>,
    pub down: Option<fn(&Connection) -> Result<()>>,
}

// Example migration
pub static V002_ADD_EVENT_METADATA: Migration = Migration {
    version: 2,
    name: "add_event_metadata",
    up: |conn| {
        conn.execute_batch("
            ALTER TABLE events ADD COLUMN metadata TEXT;
            CREATE INDEX idx_events_metadata ON events(json_extract(metadata, '$.type'));
        ")?;
        Ok(())
    },
    down: Some(|conn| {
        conn.execute_batch("
            DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_events_metadata;
            ALTER TABLE events DROP COLUMN metadata;
        ")?;
        Ok(())
    }),
};
```

### Migration Runner
```rust
pub struct Migrator {
    migrations: Vec<&'static Migration>,
}

impl Migrator {
    pub fn run(&self, conn: &Connection) -> Result<MigrationReport> {
        let current = self.get_current_version(conn)?;
        let target = self.latest_version();
        
        tracing::info\!(
            current_version = current,
            target_version = target,
            "Starting migration"
        );
        
        if current == target {
            return Ok(MigrationReport::AlreadyCurrent);
        }
        
        // Run migrations in transaction
        conn.execute("BEGIN EXCLUSIVE", [])?;
        
        for migration in &self.migrations {
            if migration.version > current {
                tracing::info\!(
                    version = migration.version,
                    name = migration.name,
                    "Running migration"
                );
                
                (migration.up)(conn)?;
                self.record_migration(conn, migration)?;
            }
        }
        
        conn.execute("COMMIT", [])?;
        
        Ok(MigrationReport::Migrated {
            from: current,
            to: target,
        })
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# Automatic on startup (default)
$ wa watch
Migrating database: v1 → v3
  Running: v002_add_event_metadata... OK
  Running: v003_fts_improvements... OK
Migration complete.

# Manual migration
$ wa db migrate
Current version: 1
Target version: 3
Run migrations? [y/N]

# Check status without migrating
$ wa db migrate --status
Current version: 1
Available migrations:
  ✓ v001_initial_schema (applied)
  ○ v002_add_event_metadata (pending)
  ○ v003_fts_improvements (pending)
```

## Safety
- All migrations run in transaction
- Backup created before migration (if large changes)
- Down migrations available for rollback
- Version tracked in dedicated table

## Testing
- Unit tests: each migration up/down
- Integration: migrate from v1 to vN
- E2E: upgrade simulation with real data

## Acceptance Criteria
- Migrations run automatically on startup
- Failed migration rolls back cleanly
- Migration status visible via CLI
- Down migrations available for emergencies

 {"id":"wa-ye8","title":"Enriched error messages: every error includes actionable remediation","description":"# Task: Enriched Error Messages

## Goal
Transform all wa error messages to include **actionable remediation steps**. Errors become guides, not dead ends.

## Why This Matters
Cryptic errors waste user time and create frustration:
- "Error: DB locked" → What do I do?
- "Error: Pane not found" → Which panes exist?
- "Error: Send denied" → Why? How do I fix it?

Enriched errors answer the "now what?" question immediately.

## Error Message Standard
Every error must include:
1. **What happened**: Clear description
2. **Why it happened**: Context/cause (when knowable)
3. **How to fix it**: Specific, copy-pasteable commands (prefer safe commands)
4. **Learn more**: Link to docs or `wa why` command

## Examples

### Before
```
Error: DB locked
```

### After
```
Error: Cannot start watcher - workspace lock is held

Another wa process may already be running.

To fix:
  - See who is running it: wa doctor
  - Stop the watcher safely: wa stop
  - If the lock appears stale, follow wa doctor's instructions (do not guess)

Learn more:
  - wa why (for decision/audit explanations)
  - wa doctor (for environment + workspace paths)
```

### Before
```
Error: Pane 99 not found
```

### After
```
Error: Pane 99 not found

Available panes:
  1: codex @ /home/user/project
  3: claude @ /home/user/other
  7: gemini @ /home/user/third

Did you mean: wa send --pane 9 "hello" (closest match)

To list all panes: wa status
```

### Before
```
Error: Send denied
```

### After
```
Error: Send denied - pane is in AltScreen mode

Pane 3 (claude_code) is currently running a full-screen application.
Sending input now could corrupt the application state.

To fix:
  - Wait for the application to exit
  - Check pane state: wa status --pane 3

To understand:
  - wa why denied --pane 3

To proceed if you truly must override:
  - use the allow-once approval flow (wa will provide a short code)
  - run: wa approve <code>
  - retry the original action
```

## Implementation

### 1. Error Type with Remediation
```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum WaError {
    #[error("Cannot start watcher - workspace lock is held")]
    WorkspaceLockHeld {
        lock_path: PathBuf,
        remediation: Vec<Remediation>,
    },

    #[error("Pane {pane_id} not found")]
    PaneNotFound {
        pane_id: PaneId,
        available_panes: Vec<PaneInfo>,
        closest_match: Option<PaneId>,
        remediation: Vec<Remediation>,
    },
    // ... more variants
}

pub enum Remediation {
    Command { description: &'static str, command: String },
    Url { description: &'static str, url: &'static str },
    Hint { text: String },
}
```

### 2. Error Formatting
```rust
impl WaError {
    pub fn display_rich(&self) -> String {
        let mut output = String::new();
        output.push_str(&format!("Error: {}\n\n", self));

        if let Some(context) = self.context() {
            output.push_str(&format!("{}\n\n", context));
        }

        output.push_str("To fix:\n");
        for remedy in self.remediation() {
            output.push_str(&format!("  - {}\n", remedy));
        }

        output
    }
}
```

### 3. Context-Aware Remediation
Use runtime context to generate specific commands:
- include actual paths, pane IDs, timestamps
- detect platform for OS-specific commands
- show closest matches for typos

### 4. Integration Points
- CLI error handler uses `display_rich()`
- Robot mode errors include remediation in JSON
- MCP errors include remediation hints

## Testing
- Unit tests: Each error type has valid remediation
- Integration tests: Errors include context-specific values
- Snapshot tests: Error messages match expected format
- UX tests: Remediation steps actually work

## Acceptance Criteria
- All error messages include "To fix:" with specific steps
- Context-specific values (paths, IDs) are included
- Remediation commands can be copy-pasted directly
- Error messages are consistent in format

 {"id":"wa-z0e","title":"[EPIC] Session Recording and Replay: capture, store, and replay agent sessions","description":"# [EPIC] Session Recording and Replay

## Mission
Enable users to record complete terminal sessions, replay them for analysis/debugging, and share them for collaboration/documentation.

## Why This Matters
Current state: wa captures segments and events, but users cannot:
- See a full session from start to finish
- Replay what happened to understand issues
- Share a recording with others
- Use recordings for demos or documentation

Session recording solves all these:
- **Debugging**: "What exactly happened before the crash?"
- **Collaboration**: "Here's a recording of the issue"
- **Documentation**: "Watch this recording to learn the workflow"
- **Compliance**: "Full audit trail of agent actions"

## Core Insight: Time-Travel Debugging
Recordings are like git for terminal sessions—you can move backward and forward through time, seeing exactly what happened at each moment.

## Scope

### Recording
- Start/stop recording for specific panes
- Automatic recording based on rules (e.g., "record all Codex panes")
- Metadata capture (timestamps, pane info, agent type)
- Efficient storage (delta-compressed)

### Replay
- Terminal-accurate playback at configurable speed
- Pause, seek, speed control
- Event annotations shown during replay
- Workflow step markers

### Export
- Self-contained recording files (.war = wa recording)
- Shareable without wa installed (HTML player option)
- Privacy controls (redact sensitive content)

### Integration
- `wa record start --pane 0`
- `wa record stop`
- `wa replay session-123.war`
- `wa replay --speed 2x`
- TUI replay viewer

## Success Criteria
- Recording adds <5% overhead to capture
- Replay is terminal-accurate (escape sequences preserved)
- Recordings can be shared and played back
- Users report recordings help with debugging

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for delta compression
- Integration tests for record/replay roundtrip
- E2E tests for full workflow recording
- Fuzz tests for malformed recording files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Recording start/stop commands functional
- [ ] Recordings stored efficiently (delta-compressed)
- [ ] Replay command plays back accurately
- [ ] Speed control works (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x)
- [ ] Event annotations visible during replay
- [ ] Export to shareable format works
- [ ] Privacy redaction configurable
- [ ] Tests cover all functionality","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:48:22.423132786Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:40:40.168154548Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-export","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-z0e","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"wa-z0e.1","title":"Recording format spec: .war file structure, delta encoding, metadata schema","description":"# Recording format specification

## Purpose
Define the .war (wa recording) file format for storing terminal sessions with efficient delta compression and rich metadata.

## Background
A good recording format must:
- Be space-efficient (delta encoding)
- Preserve timing accurately
- Include metadata for context
- Be forward-compatible (versioned)
- Be parseable without wa

## Format Structure

### File Header
```rust
pub struct WarHeader {
    magic: [u8; 4],        // "WAR\x01"
    version: u16,          // Format version
    created_at: i64,       // Unix timestamp
    pane_info: PaneSnapshot,
    metadata: RecordingMetadata,
    compression: Compression,
}

pub struct RecordingMetadata {
    title: Option<String>,
    description: Option<String>,
    agent_type: Option<AgentType>,
    tags: Vec<String>,
    total_duration_ms: u64,
    frame_count: u64,
}
```

### Frame Format
```rust
pub struct Frame {
    timestamp_ms: u64,      // Relative to recording start
    frame_type: FrameType,
    payload: Vec<u8>,
}

pub enum FrameType {
    Output(OutputFrame),    // Terminal output delta
    Resize(ResizeFrame),    // Terminal size change
    Event(EventFrame),      // wa detection event
    Marker(MarkerFrame),    // User annotation
    Input(InputFrame),      // Optional: captured input (redactable)
}

pub struct OutputFrame {
    delta: DeltaEncoding,   // Compressed diff from previous
    raw_bytes: usize,       // Original size for stats
}
```

### Delta Encoding
Use rolling hash + LZ4 for efficient storage:
```rust
pub enum DeltaEncoding {
    Full(Vec<u8>),              // Full frame (first or after discontinuity)
    Diff { base: u32, ops: Vec<DiffOp> }, // Delta from frame N
    Repeat(u32),                 // Same as previous (common for idle)
}
```

### Index Section
For fast seeking:
```rust
pub struct FrameIndex {
    keyframes: Vec<(u64, u64)>,  // (timestamp_ms, file_offset)
    events: Vec<(u64, EventId)>, // Event timestamps for annotation
}
```

## File Layout
```
[Header: 256 bytes]
[Metadata: variable, JSON]
[Frames: compressed stream]
[Index: variable]
[Footer: checksum + offsets]
```

## Versioning
- v1: Initial format
- Header includes version for forward compatibility
- Readers should reject unknown versions gracefully

## Testing
- Unit tests for encode/decode roundtrip
- Property tests for delta encoding correctness
- Fuzz tests for malformed file handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Format spec documented
- [ ] Header structure defined
- [ ] Frame types enumerated
- [ ] Delta encoding specified
- [ ] Index structure for seeking
- [ ] Version handling documented
- [ ] Tests for format parsing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:48:40.277718255Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:40:40.174661121Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-export","area-storage","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.1","depends_on_id":"wa-z0e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"wa-z0e.2","title":"Recording engine: capture pane output, compress deltas, write frames","description":"# Recording engine

## Purpose
Implement the core recording engine that captures pane output, compresses it efficiently, and writes frames to storage.

## Implementation Details

### Recorder State
```rust
pub struct Recorder {
    pane_id: u64,
    output_path: PathBuf,
    writer: FrameWriter,
    state: RecorderState,
    
    // For delta encoding
    last_frame: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    rolling_hash: RollingHash,
    
    // Stats
    frames_written: u64,
    bytes_raw: u64,
    bytes_compressed: u64,
}

pub enum RecorderState {
    Idle,
    Recording { started_at: Instant },
    Paused { duration_so_far: Duration },
    Stopped,
}
```

### Frame Capture
Hook into ingest pipeline:
```rust
impl Recorder {
    pub fn on_output_delta(&mut self, delta: &OutputDelta) -> Result<()> {
        if !self.is_recording() { return Ok(()); }
        
        let frame = self.encode_frame(delta)?;
        self.writer.write_frame(frame)?;
        self.update_stats(delta.len(), frame.len());
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn encode_frame(&mut self, delta: &OutputDelta) -> Result<Frame> {
        let timestamp = self.elapsed_ms();
        
        let encoding = if let Some(last) = &self.last_frame {
            DeltaEncoding::diff(last, &delta.bytes, &mut self.rolling_hash)
        } else {
            DeltaEncoding::Full(delta.bytes.clone())
        };
        
        self.last_frame = Some(delta.bytes.clone());
        Ok(Frame::output(timestamp, encoding))
    }
}
```

### Event Integration
Capture wa events as annotations:
```rust
impl Recorder {
    pub fn on_event(&mut self, event: &Detection) -> Result<()> {
        if !self.is_recording() { return Ok(()); }
        
        let frame = Frame::event(self.elapsed_ms(), EventFrame {
            event_id: event.id,
            event_type: event.event_type.clone(),
            summary: event.summary.clone(),
        });
        
        self.writer.write_frame(frame)
    }
}
```

### Buffered Writing
Use async writer to avoid blocking ingest:
```rust
pub struct FrameWriter {
    buffer: Vec<Frame>,
    file: BufWriter<File>,
    flush_threshold: usize,
}

impl FrameWriter {
    pub fn write_frame(&mut self, frame: Frame) -> Result<()> {
        self.buffer.push(frame);
        if self.buffer.len() >= self.flush_threshold {
            self.flush()?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

## Testing
- Unit tests for delta encoding
- Integration tests with ingest pipeline
- Benchmark tests for overhead measurement

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Recorder captures all pane output
- [ ] Delta encoding reduces storage significantly
- [ ] Events are captured as annotations
- [ ] Recording overhead <5% (benchmark verified)
- [ ] Buffered writing prevents blocking
- [ ] Tests cover all recorder states","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:48:54.690121244Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:40:40.181786429Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-ingest","area-storage","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.2","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.2","depends_on_id":"wa-z0e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}

+{"id":"wa-z0e.2","title":"Recording engine: capture pane output, compress deltas, write frames","description":"# Recording engine

## Purpose
Implement the core recording engine that captures pane output, compresses it efficiently, and writes frames to storage.

## Implementation Details

### Recorder State
```rust
pub struct Recorder {
    pane_id: u64,
    output_path: PathBuf,
    writer: FrameWriter,
    state: RecorderState,
    
    // For delta encoding
    last_frame: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    rolling_hash: RollingHash,
    
    // Stats
    frames_written: u64,
    bytes_raw: u64,
    bytes_compressed: u64,
}

pub enum RecorderState {
    Idle,
    Recording { started_at: Instant },
    Paused { duration_so_far: Duration },
    Stopped,
}
```

### Frame Capture
Hook into ingest pipeline:
```rust
impl Recorder {
    pub fn on_output_delta(&mut self, delta: &OutputDelta) -> Result<()> {
        if !self.is_recording() { return Ok(()); }
        
        let frame = self.encode_frame(delta)?;
        self.writer.write_frame(frame)?;
        self.update_stats(delta.len(), frame.len());
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn encode_frame(&mut self, delta: &OutputDelta) -> Result<Frame> {
        let timestamp = self.elapsed_ms();
        
        let encoding = if let Some(last) = &self.last_frame {
            DeltaEncoding::diff(last, &delta.bytes, &mut self.rolling_hash)
        } else {
            DeltaEncoding::Full(delta.bytes.clone())
        };
        
        self.last_frame = Some(delta.bytes.clone());
        Ok(Frame::output(timestamp, encoding))
    }
}
```

### Event Integration
Capture wa events as annotations:
```rust
impl Recorder {
    pub fn on_event(&mut self, event: &Detection) -> Result<()> {
        if !self.is_recording() { return Ok(()); }
        
        let frame = Frame::event(self.elapsed_ms(), EventFrame {
            event_id: event.id,
            event_type: event.event_type.clone(),
            summary: event.summary.clone(),
        });
        
        self.writer.write_frame(frame)
    }
}
```

### Buffered Writing
Use async writer to avoid blocking ingest:
```rust
pub struct FrameWriter {
    buffer: Vec<Frame>,
    file: BufWriter<File>,
    flush_threshold: usize,
}

impl FrameWriter {
    pub fn write_frame(&mut self, frame: Frame) -> Result<()> {
        self.buffer.push(frame);
        if self.buffer.len() >= self.flush_threshold {
            self.flush()?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

## Testing
- Unit tests for delta encoding
- Integration tests with ingest pipeline
- Benchmark tests for overhead measurement

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Recorder captures all pane output
- [ ] Delta encoding reduces storage significantly
- [ ] Events are captured as annotations
- [ ] Recording overhead <5% (benchmark verified)
- [ ] Buffered writing prevents blocking
- [ ] Tests cover all recorder states","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:48:54.690121244Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:58.267431948Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-ingest","area-storage","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.2","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.2","depends_on_id":"wa-z0e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-z0e.3","title":"Replay engine: parse recordings, seek, playback with speed control","description":"# Replay engine

## Purpose
Implement the playback engine that reads recordings and replays them with accurate timing, speed control, and seeking.

## Implementation Details

### Player State
```rust
pub struct Player {
    recording: Recording,
    position: PlaybackPosition,
    speed: f32,  // 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 4.0
    state: PlayerState,
    output_sink: Box<dyn OutputSink>,
}

pub struct PlaybackPosition {
    frame_index: usize,
    timestamp_ms: u64,
}

pub enum PlayerState {
    Playing,
    Paused,
    Stopped,
    SeekingTo(u64),
}
```

### Frame Decoding
```rust
impl Player {
    pub fn decode_frame(&self, index: usize) -> Result<DecodedFrame> {
        let frame = &self.recording.frames[index];
        
        match &frame.frame_type {
            FrameType::Output(output) => {
                let bytes = match &output.delta {
                    DeltaEncoding::Full(b) => b.clone(),
                    DeltaEncoding::Diff { base, ops } => {
                        let base_frame = self.get_base_frame(*base)?;
                        apply_diff(&base_frame, ops)
                    }
                    DeltaEncoding::Repeat(n) => self.get_frame_output(*n)?,
                };
                Ok(DecodedFrame::Output(bytes))
            }
            FrameType::Event(event) => {
                Ok(DecodedFrame::Event(event.clone()))
            }
            // ... other frame types
        }
    }
}
```

### Playback Loop
```rust
impl Player {
    pub async fn play(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        while let Some(frame) = self.next_frame() {
            // Calculate delay based on speed
            let delay = (frame.timestamp_ms - self.position.timestamp_ms) as f32 / self.speed;
            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(delay as u64)).await;
            
            // Handle pause/stop
            if self.check_controls().await? == Control::Stop {
                break;
            }
            
            // Output frame
            self.output_frame(&frame).await?;
            self.position = PlaybackPosition::from(&frame);
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Seeking
Use keyframe index for fast seeking:
```rust
impl Player {
    pub fn seek_to(&mut self, timestamp_ms: u64) -> Result<()> {
        // Find nearest keyframe before target
        let keyframe = self.recording.index.keyframes
            .iter()
            .rev()
            .find(|(ts, _)| *ts <= timestamp_ms)
            .ok_or(Error::SeekFailed)?;
        
        // Replay from keyframe to target (fast, no delays)
        self.position = PlaybackPosition::from_offset(keyframe.1);
        while self.position.timestamp_ms < timestamp_ms {
            self.advance_frame_fast()?;
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Output Sinks
```rust
pub trait OutputSink {
    fn write(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<()>;
    fn show_event(&mut self, event: &EventFrame) -> Result<()>;
    fn show_marker(&mut self, marker: &MarkerFrame) -> Result<()>;
}

// Terminal output sink (direct playback)
// TUI sink (with controls overlay)
// Headless sink (for testing)
```

## Testing
- Unit tests for frame decoding and diff application.
- Deterministic timing tests using paused time (e.g., `tokio::time::pause` + `advance`) or an injected clock; **do not** use wall-clock sleeps in tests.
- Integration tests for seeking correctness across keyframe boundaries and for speed-control behavior.


## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Playback reproduces terminal output accurately
- [ ] Speed control (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x) works
- [ ] Pause/resume works
- [ ] Seeking is fast (<100ms for any position)
- [ ] Events shown as annotations during playback
- [ ] Tests verify timing accuracy","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:49:11.872963219Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:40:40.189200602Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.3","depends_on_id":"wa-z0e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-z0e.4","title":"Recording CLI commands: wa record start/stop/list, wa replay","description":"# Recording CLI commands

## Purpose
Provide user-friendly CLI commands for recording and replaying terminal sessions.

## Commands

### wa record start
```bash
wa record start [--pane <ID>] [--title "Session name"] [--auto-stop <duration>]

# Examples
wa record start --pane 0 --title "Debugging usage limit"
wa record start --pane 0 --auto-stop 30m
```

Output:
```
Recording started for pane 0
Recording ID: rec-2026-01-18-001
Press Ctrl+C or run `wa record stop` to end
```

### wa record stop
```bash
wa record stop [--pane <ID>] [--all]

# Examples
wa record stop --pane 0
wa record stop --all
```

Output:
```
Recording stopped
Duration: 5m 23s
Frames: 1,234
Size: 256 KB (compressed from 1.2 MB)
Saved: ~/.local/share/wa/recordings/rec-2026-01-18-001.war
```

### wa record list
```bash
wa record list [--limit <N>] [--format json|table]

# Output
ID                      Duration  Size    Pane  Title
rec-2026-01-18-001      5m 23s    256KB   0     Debugging usage limit
rec-2026-01-17-003      12m 45s   512KB   1     Codex workflow test
```

### wa replay
```bash
wa replay <recording-id-or-path> [options]

Options:
  --speed <0.5|1|2|4>     Playback speed (default: 1)
  --from <timestamp>      Start from timestamp (e.g., "1m30s")
  --to <timestamp>        Stop at timestamp
  --events-only          Show only event annotations
  --tui                  Use TUI player with controls

# Examples
wa replay rec-2026-01-18-001
wa replay rec-2026-01-18-001 --speed 2 --from 2m
wa replay ./my-recording.war --tui
```

### wa record info
```bash
wa record info <recording-id-or-path>

# Output
Recording: rec-2026-01-18-001
Title: Debugging usage limit
Created: 2026-01-18 10:15:00
Duration: 5m 23s
Frames: 1,234
Size: 256 KB
Compression: 78%
Pane: 0 (zsh @ /home/user/project)
Agent: Codex
Events: 3 (compaction, usage_warning, usage_limit)
```

## Robot Mode
```bash
wa robot record start --pane 0
wa robot record stop
wa robot record list
wa robot replay <id> --headless --output events.jsonl
```

## Testing
- CLI argument parsing tests
- Integration tests for record/stop/replay flow
- Output format tests (table, JSON)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] wa record start works with all options
- [ ] wa record stop gracefully ends recording
- [ ] wa record list shows recordings with metadata
- [ ] wa replay plays back accurately
- [ ] wa record info shows detailed metadata
- [ ] Robot mode commands produce valid JSON
- [ ] Tests cover all commands","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:49:30.449876410Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:40:40.196788232Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.4","depends_on_id":"wa-z0e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.4","depends_on_id":"wa-z0e.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.4","depends_on_id":"wa-z0e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.4","depends_on_id":"wa-z0e.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-z0e.5","title":"Recording export: HTML player, privacy redaction, sharing","description":"# Recording export and sharing

## Purpose
Enable users to share recordings with others, including an HTML player for those without wa installed, and privacy controls for sensitive content.

## Export Formats

### Standalone HTML Player
```bash
wa record export <id> --format html --output recording.html

# Creates self-contained HTML file with embedded player
# Uses asciinema-player or custom player
# No server required - opens in any browser
```

HTML player features:
- Terminal-accurate rendering
- Speed control (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x)
- Pause/seek/play
- Event annotations as clickable markers
- Responsive design

### Asciinema Format
```bash
wa record export <id> --format asciinema --output recording.cast

# Compatible with asciinema.org and asciinema-player
```

### GIF Export
```bash
wa record export <id> --format gif --output recording.gif

# For documentation and quick sharing
# Configurable size, frame rate, quality
```

## Privacy Redaction

### Redaction Rules
```rust
pub struct RedactionConfig {
    patterns: Vec<RedactionPattern>,
    mode: RedactionMode,
}

pub enum RedactionMode {
    Mask,       // Replace with ***
    Remove,     // Delete entirely
    Placeholder(String), // Replace with custom text
}

pub struct RedactionPattern {
    pattern: Regex,
    description: String,  // "API keys", "passwords"
}
```

### Built-in Patterns
- API keys (sk-*, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.)
- Passwords (password=*, --password)
- Tokens (Bearer *, token=*)
- Personal info (email patterns, IP addresses)

### Usage
```bash
wa record export <id> --redact-secrets --output safe-recording.war
wa record export <id> --redact-pattern "CUSTOM_SECRET_\w+" --output safe.war
```

## Sharing Workflow
```bash
# Export with redaction
wa record export rec-001 --redact-secrets --format html --output debug-session.html

# Share the file
# User opens in browser - no wa installation needed
```

## Testing
- HTML player renders correctly in major browsers
- Redaction removes sensitive content
- Export/import roundtrip preserves non-redacted content
- GIF export produces valid files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HTML export creates self-contained player
- [ ] HTML player works in Chrome, Firefox, Safari
- [ ] Asciinema format compatible with player
- [ ] GIF export produces reasonable quality
- [ ] Redaction removes secrets from output
- [ ] Custom redaction patterns work
- [ ] Tests verify redaction completeness","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:49:46.670996950Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:40:40.204223965Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-export","area-privacy","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-z0e.5","depends_on_id":"wa-z0e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}
